segments.text: My spirit is broken, My days are extinguished, The grave is ready for me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are not mockers with me? And does not my eye dwell on their provocation?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now put down a pledge for me with Yourself. Who is he who will shake hands with me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For You have hidden their heart from understanding; Therefore You will not exalt them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He who speaks flattery to his friends, Even the eyes of his children will fail.
But He has made me a byword of the people, And I have become one in whose face men spit.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My eye has also grown dim because of sorrow, And all my members are like shadows.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Upright men are astonished at this, And the innocent stirs himself up against the hypocrite.
Yet the righteous will hold to his way, And he who has clean hands will be stronger and stronger.
But please, come back again, all of you, For I shall not find one wise man among you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My days are past, My purposes are broken off, Even the thoughts of my heart.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They change the night into day; 'The light is near,' they say, in the face of darkness.
If I wait for the grave as my house, If I make my bed in the darkness,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I say to corruption, 'You are my father,' And to the worm, 'You are my mother and my sister,'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Where then is my hope? As for my hope, who can see it?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will they go down to the gates of Sheol? Shall we have rest together in the dust?
segments.speaker: Job
